Paragraph.Sentence: Weekly attendance figures for a movie shown at a theater are given in the chart below. The theater will start making a profit when weekly attendance goes above 4,000.
If this attendance pattern continues, during which week will attendance first go above 4,000?

response.feedback: Sorry, that's not right. The attendance for week 5 does not go above 4,000.

The student may have made an arithmetic error in generating the next term for this geometric sequence, or may have an incomplete understanding of how to generate additional terms in a geometric sequence.
The student's response is consistent with an understanding of how to generate successive terms in a geometric sequence.
Sorry, that's incorrect. Although you are correct that the weekly attendance is greater than 4,000 in week 7, this is not the first week in which this occurs.

The student may have misinterpreted the question, and selected any week for which the attendance is greater than 4,000, rather than the first week for which the attendance is greater than 4,000. It is also possible that the student has an incomplete understanding of how to generate successive terms in a geometric sequence.
Sorry, that's incorrect. Although you are correct that the weekly attendance is greater than 4,000 in week 8, this is not the first week in which this occurs.

The student may have misinterpreted the question, and selected any week for which the attendance is greater than 4,000, rather than the first week for which the attendance is greater than 4,000. It is also possible that the student has an incomplete understanding of how to generate successive terms in a geometric sequence..
